document.title: Post Market Surveillance Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Post Market Surveillance Report Rev. 1 for ADD Abyz Digital Detector, documenting PMS sources, adverse event and recall searches, PMS results, FSCA/FSN handling, and PMCF determination.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co. Ltd.
keyPoints: PMS was conducted in accordance with PMS Plan Doc. No. A-PMS-003.
The device is ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, not for mammography.
The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
PMS information sources included literature reviews, media, and experience with similar devices.
The report concluded no additional measures such as product design change and process improvement were required after post-2020.06 market research analysis.
The report states PMCF does not need to be performed because all listed cases were not met by PMS activity data.

evidence.claim: PMS activities used literature reviews, media, and experience with similar devices as selected sources from NB-MED-2.12/1.
evidence.evidenceText: To collect data, we selected three sources from the PMS sources listed in 'NB-MED-2.12 / 1'. 1. Literature Reviews 2. The media 3. Experience with similar devices made by the same or different manufacturer

evidence.claim: The literature search covered 2000 to 2019 and was searched from 2020.01.05 to 2020.06.15.
evidence.evidenceText: Date of Search 2020.01.05 ~ 2020.06.15 ... Period Covered by Search From 2000 to 2019 years

evidence.claim: The literature sources were PUBMED, European Radiology, and Medical Physics.
evidence.evidenceText: Literature Source PUBMED, European Radiology, Medical Physics

evidence.claim: Eight literatures were used for the clinical evaluation report.
evidence.evidenceText: 8 literatures used for this clinical evaluation report.

evidence.claim: Media searches using detector-related keywords in Google and Yahoo found nothing applicable.
evidence.evidenceText: We searched as keyword such as ‘Solid State X-Ray Imager’, ‘Digital Imager’, ‘Flat panel digital X-ray detector’, ‘digital detector’, ‘Flat panel detector’, ‘detector’, ‘X-ray detector’, ‘FPD’ in google and yahoo, it is not found applicable

evidence.claim: Korea, Europe, and USA similar-device searches were performed on 2020.08.05.
evidence.evidenceText: Nation Search Date Korea ... 2020.08.05 Europe ... 2020.08.05 USA ... 2020.08.05

evidence.claim: The Korea search found no adverse event or recall related to the Digital Flat Panel X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: There is no adverse event or recall related to the Digital Flat Panel X-ray detector in the Korea

evidence.claim: The Europe similar-device review concluded no case related to recall and accident report was encountered.
evidence.evidenceText: Conclusion - As a result of confirming the accident report of similar equipment, we have not encountered any case related to recall and accident report.

evidence.claim: USA MAUDE event SD-16 was related to a similar device and referred to the Clinical Evaluation Report.
evidence.evidenceText: This event is related to similar device. It is explained in clinical evaluation report Cl)4. Refer to Clinical Evaluation Report (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-060)
evidence.pageTarget: USA MAUDE Similar Device Event SD-16

evidence.claim: After post-2020.06 market research analysis, no additional measures such as product design change or process improvement were required.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of the post-2020.06 market research analysis, no additional measures such as product design change and process improvement are required.

evidence.claim: Corrective and preventive measures were not carried out for this PMS analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, follow-up (corrective and preventive measures) to the post-market research conducted this time are not carried out.

evidence.claim: For serious adverse events and accidents with the product, FSCA will be implemented according to MEDDEV 2.12 Post-Market Surveillance.
evidence.evidenceText: In the event of serious adverse events and an accident with our product, Field Safety Corrective Action will be implemented in accordance with MEDDEV 2.12 Post-Market Surveillance.

evidence.claim: The FSCA and FSN forms follow MEDDEV 2.12 Post Market Surveillance templates.
evidence.evidenceText: The FSCA and FSN form is according to Field safety corrective action – FSCA and Field safety notice template in MEDDEV 2.12 Post Market surveillance.

evidence.claim: PMCF was determined not to be needed.
evidence.evidenceText: But all the cases are not met to PMS activity data. So, the PMCF does not need to be performed.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata is broad: source metadata says authoritySignal is general, while the report references MDD, MEDDEV, competent authorities, notified bodies, Korea, Europe, USA, MFDS, FDA MAUDE, and MHRA.
Page 1 shows Rev. No. 0 Date 2020.06.28, while later pages and revision history show Rev. No. 1 Date 2021.01.29; document fields use Rev. No. 1 based on the revision history and pages 2-19.

PMCF section contains an apparent device inconsistency: item 3 says 'Our device is a product that pushes the limbs and helps the blood to circulate well', conflicting with the device information identifying ADD as a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector.
OCR/text quality issues are present, including malformed phrases such as 'clinical evaluation report Cl)4', line breaks in URLs, and awkward translated wording.
